doctrine: Mission overlays change constantly; role doctrines rarely; provider bindings are replaceable. This is the overlay contract. SCOPE and TOOLS_ALLOWED are allowlists — anything not named is denied.

truth_source_projection.note: TRUTH_SOURCE names the MECHANISM. It projects onto the founder-ratified truth classification in backend/security/proof_contract.py. It is never a second truth vocabulary. Only OBSERVED and DERIVED are in ADVANCING.

invariants: Every declared FOUNDER_GATE requires a stop condition mentioning the founder gate — declaring a gate never authorizes it.
EDR_REQUIRED=YES obliges an EDR path in EXPECTED_OUTPUTS before the mission may return DONE.
A mission may not return DONE on a non-advancing TRUTH_SOURCE (STATIC_ANALYSIS, HUMAN_INPUT, UNKNOWN).
SCOPE and TOOLS_ALLOWED are allowlists; an empty allowlist denies everything and is a validation error, not a wildcard.
Validation fails CLOSED: an unparseable or incomplete mission is BLOCKED, never best-effort.
EXECUTION_CONTEXT must carry correlation_id, never a forked run_id.

execution_context.note: Immutable execution fingerprint captured at mission ISSUE time. Reuses the INCUMBENT correlation_id from backend/helm_runtime/transaction.py — a separate run_id would fork the identifier vocabulary. transaction_id and mission_version have a different lifecycle (minted at WRITE time) and are not declared here.
execution_context.honesty_rule: A field that cannot be determined is recorded as UNKNOWN. It is never guessed, defaulted, or back-filled.
execution_context.reproducibility: An UNKNOWN commit_sha or a dirty working tree means evidence produced under this context cannot be reproduced from the repository alone. This is REPORTED, not blocked — a blocking rule would halt every mission during active development.
